content.text: Secrets Scanning CI Gates for AI Repos

AI repositories are high-value targets for secret scanning because they accumulate credentials from multiple third-party services: LLM provider API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google), vector database credentials (Pinecone, Weaviate), evaluation platform keys (LangSmith, Helicone), and cloud provider credentials for model hosting. A single leaked LLM API key can cost thousands of dollars in API charges within hours.

Defense-in-depth means three layers: a pre-commit hook that blocks secrets before they enter git history, a CI gate on every PR that catches anything that slipped through, and a repo-level full-history scan that catches secrets committed before the gates were in place.

attrs.alt: Three-layer secrets scanning defense: pre-commit, CI gate, repo-level full history scan

content.text: Layer 1: Pre-commit Hook with Gitleaks and detect-secrets
Pre-commit hooks run locally before each commit. They are the fastest feedback loop — the developer sees the failure immediately, before the secret ever touches the remote. Use both

# .pre-commit-config.yaml
repos:
  # Gitleaks: high-recall regex scanner for 150+ secret patterns
  - repo: https://github.com/gitleaks/gitleaks
    rev: v8.18.2
    hooks:
      - id: gitleaks
        args: ["--config", ".gitleaks.toml"]

  # detect-secrets: baseline-aware scanner with allowlist support
  - repo: https://github.com/Yelp/detect-secrets
    rev: v1.4.0
    hooks:
      - id: detect-secrets
        args: ["--baseline", ".secrets.baseline"]
        exclude: |
          (?x)^(
            tests/fixtures/mock_responses.json|
            docs/examples/.*
          )$

# .gitleaks.toml — add AI-specific patterns beyond the built-in ruleset
title = "GenAI Repository Secret Scanner"

[extend]
useDefault = true  # include all built-in gitleaks rules

[[rules]]
id = "langsmith-api-key"
description = "LangSmith API key"
regex = '''ls-[a-zA-Z0-9]{40,}'''
keywords = ["langsmith", "LANGSMITH_API_KEY"]

[[rules]]
id = "langfuse-secret-key"
description = "Langfuse secret key"
regex = '''sk-lf-[a-zA-Z0-9\-]{30,}'''
keywords = ["langfuse", "LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY"]

[[rules]]
id = "helicone-api-key"
description = "Helicone API key"
regex = '''sk-helicone-[a-zA-Z0-9\-]{30,}'''
keywords = ["helicone", "HELICONE_API_KEY"]

[[rules]]
id = "pinecone-api-key"
description = "Pinecone API key"
regex = '''[a-f0-9]{8}-[a-f0-9]{4}-[a-f0-9]{4}-[a-f0-9]{4}-[a-f0-9]{12}'''
keywords = ["pinecone", "PINECONE_API_KEY"]

[allowlist]
description = "Known test fixtures with fake secrets"
paths = [
    "tests/fixtures/",
    "docs/examples/",
]
Layer 2: CI Gate on Every Pull Request
The CI gate scans the full diff of the PR branch against the target branch. Even if a developer bypassed the pre-commit hook with

marks.type: , this gate catches the secret before it merges to main.

content.text:
# .github/workflows/secrets-scan.yml
name: Secrets Scan

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: ["main", "release/**"]
  push:
    branches: ["main"]

jobs:
  gitleaks:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0  # full history so gitleaks can scan the commit range

      - name: Run Gitleaks
        uses: gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          GITLEAKS_CONFIG: .gitleaks.toml
          GITLEAKS_ENABLE_COMMENTS: "true"  # posts PR comment with findings

  detect-secrets:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with: { python-version: "3.12" }

      - name: Install detect-secrets
        run: pip install detect-secrets

      - name: Scan diff against baseline
        run: |
          detect-secrets scan --baseline .secrets.baseline
          if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
            echo "New secrets detected beyond baseline — failing PR"
            echo "Run: detect-secrets scan > .secrets.baseline && detect-secrets audit .secrets.baseline"
            echo "Then commit the updated .secrets.baseline to mark false positives."
            exit 1
          fi

  trufflehog-diff:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with: { fetch-depth: 0 }

      - name: TruffleHog scan (PR diff only)
        uses: trufflesecurity/trufflehog@main
        with:
          base: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
          head: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          extra_args: --only-verified  # only report verified active secrets
Layer 3: Full History Scan with TruffleHog

New scanners miss secrets committed before the gate was in place. Run TruffleHog against the full git history on a schedule to surface historical leaks. Pair with GitHub Secret Scanning (push protection) for platform-level enforcement.

# One-time full history scan — run locally or in a CI job
pip install trufflehog3

# Scan the local repo's full git history
trufflehog git file://. \
  --only-verified \
  --json \
  > results/trufflehog_history.json

# Review findings
cat results/trufflehog_history.json | python3 -c "
import json, sys
for line in sys.stdin:
    try:
        r = json.loads(line)
        print(f"FOUND: {r.get('DetectorName')} in {r.get('SourceMetadata',{}).get('Data',{}).get('Git',{}).get('file','')} @ {r.get('SourceMetadata',{}).get('Data',{}).get('Git',{}).get('commit','')[:8]}")
    except Exception:
        pass
"

# .github/workflows/history-scan.yml — weekly full history scan
name: Full History Secrets Scan

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 5 * * 0"  # Sundays at 5 AM UTC
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  trufflehog-history:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with: { fetch-depth: 0 }

      - name: TruffleHog full history
        uses: trufflesecurity/trufflehog@main
        with:
          extra_args: --only-verified

      - name: Notify on findings
        if: failure()
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK: ${{ secrets.SLACK_SECURITY_WEBHOOK }}
        run: |
          python3 -c "
          import json, urllib.request, os
          msg = {'text': ':key: *Secret found in git history* — check the Security tab for details.'}
          req = urllib.request.Request(
              os.environ['SLACK_WEBHOOK'],
              data=json.dumps(msg).encode(),
              headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'}
          )
          urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10)
          "
Responding to a Leaked Secret
When a scanner finds a live secret, the response must be immediate. Rotating the key is not enough — the secret exists in git history and must be treated as permanently compromised.
1. Revoke the secret immediately at the provider (OpenAI dashboard, AWS IAM, Pinecone console). Do not wait.
2. Issue a new secret and update it in the secrets manager (AWS Secrets Manager, Vault, GitHub Secrets).
3. Audit the provider's usage logs for the period the secret was exposed — look for unexpected API calls.
4. Remove the secret from git history using git-filter-repo or BFG Repo Cleaner, then force-push all affected branches.
5. Notify affected stakeholders if the leaked key had access to user data or production systems.
6. Add the leaked pattern as a custom rule in .gitleaks.toml to ensure it is never committed again.

# Remove a secret from git history using git-filter-repo
pip install git-filter-repo

# Replace the leaked value with a placeholder in all commits
git filter-repo \
  --replace-text <(echo 'sk-abc123...==>REDACTED_API_KEY') \
  --force

# After rewriting history, force-push ALL branches
git push origin --all --force
git push origin --tags --force

# Warn all collaborators to re-clone — their local refs are now invalid
echo "IMPORTANT: All team members must re-clone the repository after this operation"
Common Pitfalls in Secrets Scanning for AI Repos
Pitfall
Problem
Fix
Only scanning the working tree
Secrets in earlier commits stay in history undetected
Use --depth 0 / full history mode in CI; run TruffleHog on history weekly
Not adding AI-specific patterns
Gitleaks built-ins miss LangSmith, Helicone, Pinecone keys
Extend .gitleaks.toml with custom rules for each AI service in use
Marking all findings as false positives in baseline
Baseline becomes a whitelist; real secrets get approved silently
Audit each baseline entry manually; require team review for new suppressions
Rotating key but not rewriting history
Revoked key is still in git history; remains a liability
Rewrite history with git-filter-repo after revoking; force-push all branches
Not scanning .env.example files
.env.example often has real values copy-pasted from working env
Include .env.example in the scan scope; never put real values in examples
Managing the detect-secrets Baseline
The

marks.type: file tracks what detect-secrets has already seen. Without it, every CI run would re-flag the same known false positives (test fixture files, example config values). The baseline must be committed to the repo, audited when updated, and reviewed in PRs that modify it.

content.text:
# First-time setup: scan and create baseline
pip install detect-secrets
detect-secrets scan > .secrets.baseline

# Interactively mark false positives as acceptable
detect-secrets audit .secrets.baseline
# For each finding:
#   y = this is a real secret (do NOT mark as FP — fix it instead)
#   n = this is a false positive (add to baseline)
#   s = skip for now

# Commit the baseline
git add .secrets.baseline
git commit -m "chore(security): initialise detect-secrets baseline"
Preventing Secrets in Jupyter Notebooks

Jupyter notebooks are a common source of secret leaks in AI repositories — API keys are often pasted directly into notebook cells during experimentation and committed with output cells that show API responses. Standard scanners may miss secrets embedded in notebook JSON.

# Install nbstripout to remove cell outputs before commit
pip install nbstripout

# Configure git to auto-strip outputs from notebooks
nbstripout --install

# Verify configuration
nbstripout --status
# → nbstripout is installed in repository /path/to/repo

# For CI: also verify no notebooks with output are committed
python3 -c "
import json, sys, glob
errors = []
for nb_path in glob.glob('**/*.ipynb', recursive=True):
    with open(nb_path) as f:
        nb = json.load(f)
    for i, cell in enumerate(nb.get('cells', [])):
        outputs = cell.get('outputs', [])
        if outputs:
            errors.append(f'{nb_path} cell {i}: has {len(outputs)} output(s) — strip before committing')
        # Also check cell source for secret patterns
        source = ''.join(cell.get('source', []))
        for pattern in ['sk-', 'sk-ant-', 'AIzaSy', 'PINECONE_API_KEY =']:
            if pattern in source:
                errors.append(f'{nb_path} cell {i}: possible secret pattern "{pattern}"')
if errors:
    for e in errors:
        print(f'ERROR: {e}')
    sys.exit(1)
print('OK: no notebooks with outputs or obvious secret patterns')
"

# Add to .pre-commit-config.yaml
  - repo: https://github.com/kynan/nbstripout
    rev: 0.7.1
    hooks:
      - id: nbstripout
        # Strips cell outputs and execution counts from all notebooks
        # This prevents output cells (which may contain API responses) from being committed
Enforcing Secret Rotation Policy

Long-lived API keys are a liability. Even if never leaked, a key used for 18 months has been seen by many systems — CI logs, deployment scripts, container images. Enforce a rotation policy by checking key age in a scheduled job and alerting before keys expire or become stale.

# scripts/check_key_rotation.py
# Reads key metadata from a rotation registry and alerts on stale keys
import json, os, sys
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from pathlib import Path

ROTATION_REGISTRY = Path("security/key-rotation-registry.json")
STALE_AFTER_DAYS = 90
WARN_BEFORE_DAYS = 14  # warn 14 days before stale threshold

if not ROTATION_REGISTRY.exists():
    print("WARNING: key-rotation-registry.json not found — creating template")
    template = {
        "keys": [
            {
                "name": "OPENAI_API_KEY",
                "last_rotated": "2025-01-01",
                "owner": "platform-team",
                "environment": "production"
            }
        ]
    }
    ROTATION_REGISTRY.parent.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    ROTATION_REGISTRY.write_text(json.dumps(template, indent=2))
    sys.exit(0)

with open(ROTATION_REGISTRY) as f:
    registry = json.load(f)

now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
alerts = []
warnings = []

for key in registry.get("keys", []):
    name = key["name"]
    last_rotated = datetime.fromisoformat(key["last_rotated"]).replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
    age_days = (now - last_rotated).days
    days_until_stale = STALE_AFTER_DAYS - age_days

    if age_days >= STALE_AFTER_DAYS:
        alerts.append(f"{name}: STALE (last rotated {age_days}d ago, owner: {key.get('owner')})")
    elif days_until_stale <= WARN_BEFORE_DAYS:
        warnings.append(f"{name}: rotation due in {days_until_stale}d (owner: {key.get('owner')})")

for w in warnings:
    print(f"WARN: {w}")

if alerts:
    for a in alerts:
        print(f"ALERT: {a}")
    sys.exit(1)

if not warnings and not alerts:
    print(f"OK: all {len(registry['keys'])} keys rotated within {STALE_AFTER_DAYS}d")
Summary

Secrets scanning for AI repositories requires three complementary layers: a pre-commit hook (gitleaks + detect-secrets) that blocks secrets before commit, a CI PR gate that catches bypasses before merge, and a weekly full-history scan (TruffleHog) that surfaces historical leaks. AI repos need custom scanner rules for LLM provider keys (

marks.type: ), vector database credentials, and evaluation platform tokens that standard rulesets do not cover.

content.text: When a live secret is found: revoke immediately, rotate, audit usage logs, rewrite git history with

marks.type: , and force-push all branches. Skipping the history rewrite leaves the revoked key permanently in the repository's commit log.
